Index-repo.description: Index using repository instructions directory (mutation enabled)
notes: Absolute INDEX_SERVER_DIR avoids workspace vs global cwd mismatch.
cwd pinned to repo so usage-snapshot.json lands under data/ and relative schema loads stay consistent.
Set INDEX_SERVER_LOG_DIAG=1 temporarily to verify startup line shows writable=true.
Disable mutation by removing INDEX_SERVER_MUTATION or setting it to empty string.

Index-external-data.description: Index with external writable data directory (keeps repo clean)
notes: Create C:/mcp-data/instructions beforehand with write permissions.
Diagnostic logging enabled on first run; set INDEX_SERVER_LOG_DIAG back to 0 afterward.
Ensures global instance does not clash with workspace instructions directory.

Index-readonly.description: Read-only mode (no mutation) referencing existing Index
notes: No writes will occur; useful for auditors or secondary viewers.
If add/import calls are attempted they will return Mutation disabled errors.

lessonsLearned: Always specify absolute INDEX_SERVER_DIR in global configs to avoid silent writes to unintended directories.
Pin cwd so relative paths (schemas/, data/) resolve predictably.
Use INDEX_SERVER_LOG_DIAG=1 on first launch to confirm instructionsDir path and writable flag.
Avoid running multiple instances pointing to different Indexs unless intentional; if sharing, point both to the exact same INDEX_SERVER_DIR.
For clean separation, dedicate an external data directory (e.g. C:/mcp-data/instructions).
